name: gitr
description: CommitPulse transforms your Git repositories into actionable engineering intelligence. It extracts commit history, links commits to Jira and Linear issues, syncs contributor data from GitHub, and calculates team-level metrics — all from within VS Code. Data is stored locally in PostgreSQL (via Docker), keeping your engineering analytics private, fast, and under your control. Public repo: https://github.com/antonajp/CommitPulse

commands.title: Gitr: Open Sprint Velocity vs LOC Chart

path.description: Absolute path to the Git repository

organization.description: Organization or team that owns this repository

trackerType.description: Issue tracker type for this repository (jira, linear, or none)

repoUrl.description: Repository URL for commit and PR linking (e.g., https://github.com/owner/repo)

startDate.description: Earliest commit date to extract (YYYY-MM-DD). Commits before this date are ignored. Overrides global sinceDate.

gitrx.repositories.description: List of repositories to analyze. Each entry requires a path and name.
gitrx.repositories.markdownDescription:
List of repositories to analyze. Each entry is an object with `path` (required), `name` (required), `organization` (optional), `trackerType` (optional: `jira`, `linear`, or `none`, defaults to `jira`), `repoUrl` (optional: URL for commit/PR linking), and `startDate` (optional: earliest date YYYY-MM-DD to extract).

Example:
```json
[
  { "path": "/home/user/repos/my-app", "name": "My App", "organization": "Engineering", "trackerType": "linear", "repoUrl": "https://github.com/myorg/my-app", "startDate": "2023-01-01" }
]
```

gitrx.database.host.markdownDescription: PostgreSQL database host. Maps from legacy `db_host` in `unclassified.properties`.

gitrx.database.port.description: PostgreSQL database port (5433 to avoid conflict with other local PostgreSQL instances)
gitrx.database.port.markdownDescription: PostgreSQL database port. Default `5433` avoids conflicts with other local PostgreSQL instances on the standard `5432` port.

gitrx.database.name.markdownDescription: PostgreSQL database name. Maps from legacy `db_name` in `unclassified.properties`.

gitrx.database.user.markdownDescription: PostgreSQL database user. Maps from legacy `db_user` in `unclassified.properties`. Password is stored securely via the **Gitr: Set Database Password** command.

gitrx.database.migrationUser.description: Optional privileged user for running database migrations (DDL). Falls back to gitrx.database.user if empty.
gitrx.database.migrationUser.markdownDescription:
Optional privileged PostgreSQL user for running database migrations (DDL operations). If left empty, the main `gitrx.database.user` is used for both migrations and pipeline queries.

Set this to a user with DDL privileges (CREATE TABLE, ALTER, DROP) while keeping `gitrx.database.user` restricted to DML (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) for privilege separation.

The migration user's password is stored securely via the **Gitr: Set Migration Database Password** command. If no migration password is set, the main database password is used.

gitrx.jira.server.markdownDescription:
Jira server URL. Maps from legacy `jira_server` in `unclassified.properties`.

Example: `https://yourorg.atlassian.net`

gitrx.jira.username.markdownDescription: Jira username (email) for API authentication. Maps from legacy `jira_username` in `unclassified.properties`. The API token is stored securely via the **Gitr: Set Jira API Token** command.

gitrx.jira.projectKeys.markdownDescription:
Jira project keys to track. Each key must be uppercase alphanumeric.

Example: `["PROJ", "FEAT", "BUG"]`

gitrx.jira.keyAliases.description: Jira key aliases for renamed projects (e.g., {"PROJ": "PROJ"})
gitrx.jira.keyAliases.markdownDescription:
Jira key aliases for renamed projects. Maps old project keys to their current names.

Example: `{ "PROJ": "PROJ2", "CRM": "CRMREO" }`

gitrx.jira.pointsField.description: Jira custom field ID for story points (e.g., customfield_10034)
gitrx.jira.pointsField.markdownDescription:
Jira custom field ID for story points. Default: `customfield_10034`.

This varies by Jira instance. Check your Jira admin settings to find the correct field ID.

gitrx.jira.maxKeys.description: Maximum issue key number to scan per project (0 = auto-detect from database)
gitrx.jira.maxKeys.markdownDescription: Maximum issue key number to scan per project. Set to `0` (default) to auto-detect the max key from the database and scan forward by a buffer. Set to a specific number to scan up to `PROJECT-N`.

gitrx.jira.increment.description: Number of issues to fetch beyond the current max issue number per incremental run
gitrx.jira.increment.markdownDescription:
Number of issues to fetch beyond the current max issue number per incremental run. During each pipeline execution, the loader scans from `maxKey` to `maxKey + increment` for each project.

Default: `200`

gitrx.jira.daysAgo.description: Number of days to look back when refreshing unfinished Jira issues
gitrx.jira.daysAgo.markdownDescription:
Number of days to look back for recently completed Jira issues during unfinished issue refresh. Issues that transitioned to Done/Cancelled within this window are also refreshed.

Default: `2`

gitrx.jira.urlPrefix.description: Base URL for Jira issue navigation (e.g., https://yourorg.atlassian.net). Falls back to gitrx.jira.server if empty.
gitrx.jira.urlPrefix.markdownDescription:
Base URL for Jira issue navigation. When set, issue links open as `{urlPrefix}/browse/{ISSUE-KEY}`.

If empty, falls back to `gitrx.jira.server/browse/`.

Example: `https://yourorg.atlassian.net`

gitrx.jira.debugLogging.description: Enable verbose debug logging for Jira API requests and responses.
gitrx.jira.debugLogging.markdownDescription:
Enable verbose debug logging for Jira API requests and responses.

When enabled, logs:
- Full API request URLs
- Request headers (auth redacted)
- JQL queries and pagination
- Response summaries and error details

**Note:** Also set `gitrx.logLevel` to `DEBUG` or `TRACE` to see the output.

gitrx.git.debugLogging.description: Enable verbose debug logging for Git extraction operations.
gitrx.git.debugLogging.markdownDescription:
Enable verbose debug logging for Git extraction operations.

When enabled, logs:
- Repository initialization and URL
- Branch and tag discovery
- Commit processing progress
- File diff statistics
- Git command execution

**Note:** Also set `gitrx.logLevel` to `DEBUG` or `TRACE` to see the output.

gitrx.linear.teamKeys.markdownDescription:
Linear team keys to track. Each key must be uppercase alphanumeric.

Example: `["ENG", "PLAT", "IQS"]`

gitrx.linear.keyAliases.description: Linear key aliases for renamed teams (e.g., {"OLD": "NEW"})
gitrx.linear.keyAliases.markdownDescription:
Linear key aliases for renamed teams. Maps old team keys to their current names.

Example: `{ "OLD": "NEW" }`

gitrx.linear.maxKeys.description: Maximum issue key number to scan per team (0 = auto-detect from database)
gitrx.linear.maxKeys.markdownDescription: Maximum issue key number to scan per team. Set to `0` (default) to auto-detect the max key from the database and scan forward by a buffer.

gitrx.linear.increment.description: Number of issues to fetch beyond the current max issue number per incremental run
gitrx.linear.increment.markdownDescription:
Number of issues to fetch beyond the current max issue number per incremental Linear run.

Default: `200`

gitrx.linear.daysAgo.description: Number of days to look back when refreshing unfinished Linear issues
gitrx.linear.daysAgo.markdownDescription:
Number of days to look back for recently completed Linear issues during unfinished issue refresh.

Default: `2`

gitrx.linear.urlPrefix.description: Base URL for Linear issue navigation (e.g., https://linear.app/yourteam). Falls back to https://linear.app/{team}/issue/ if empty.
gitrx.linear.urlPrefix.markdownDescription:
Base URL for Linear issue navigation. When set, issue links open as `{urlPrefix}/issue/{ISSUE-KEY}`.

If empty, the team key is extracted from the issue ID and the URL is built as `https://linear.app/{team}/issue/{ISSUE-KEY}`.

Example: `https://linear.app/yourteam`

gitrx.github.organization.description: GitHub organization name for repository correlation
gitrx.github.organization.markdownDescription: GitHub organization name for repository correlation. The GitHub personal access token is stored securely via the **Gitr: Set GitHub Token** command.

gitrx.schedule.enabled.markdownDescription: Enable automatic scheduled pipeline execution. When enabled, the pipeline runs on the schedule defined by `gitrx.schedule.cronExpression`.

gitrx.schedule.cronExpression.description: Cron expression for scheduled pipeline runs (default: weekdays at 9 AM)
gitrx.schedule.cronExpression.markdownDescription:
Cron expression for scheduled pipeline runs. Uses standard 5-field cron syntax.

Default: `0 9 * * 1-5` (weekdays at 9:00 AM)

Examples:
- `0 */4 * * *` — every 4 hours
- `0 9 * * 1-5` — weekdays at 9 AM
- `0 0 * * 0` — weekly on Sunday at midnight

gitrx.logLevel.description: Logging verbosity level for the Gitr output channel
gitrx.logLevel.markdownDescription:
Logging verbosity level for the Gitr output channel.

- **TRACE** — Most verbose, all internal operations
- **DEBUG** — Detailed diagnostic information
- **INFO** — General operational messages
- **WARN** — Warning conditions
- **ERROR** — Error conditions
- **CRITICAL** — Only critical failures

gitrx.pipeline.steps.description: Pipeline steps to execute. Default runs 5 core Jira steps. Set to [] (empty array) to run all 9 steps including Linear and teamAssignment.
gitrx.pipeline.steps.markdownDescription:
Pipeline steps to execute when running `Gitr: Run Pipeline`.

**Default** (5 core Jira steps): `gitCommitExtraction`, `githubContributorSync`, `jiraIssueLoading`, `jiraChangelogUpdate`, `commitJiraLinking`

Set to `[]` (empty array) to run all 9 steps including Linear integration and team assignment.

**Available steps:**
- `gitCommitExtraction` — Extract commits from Git repositories
- `githubContributorSync` — Sync contributors from GitHub
- `jiraIssueLoading` — Load new Jira issues incrementally
- `jiraChangelogUpdate` — Refresh unfinished Jira issues
- `commitJiraLinking` — Link commits to Jira issues via regex
- `linearIssueLoading` — Load new Linear issues incrementally
- `linearChangelogUpdate` — Refresh unfinished Linear issues
- `commitLinearLinking` — Link commits to Linear issues via regex
- `teamAssignment` — Calculate primary team assignments

**Examples:**
```json
// Default: 5 core Jira steps (new installs)
"gitrx.pipeline.steps": ["gitCommitExtraction", "githubContributorSync", "jiraIssueLoading", "jiraChangelogUpdate", "commitJiraLinking"]

// All 9 steps (Linear users, team assignment)
"gitrx.pipeline.steps": []

// Custom subset
"gitrx.pipeline.steps": ["gitCommitExtraction", "commitJiraLinking"]
```

gitrx.pipeline.sinceDate.description: Earliest commit date to extract (YYYY-MM-DD). Commits before this date are ignored. Per-repo startDate takes precedence.
gitrx.pipeline.sinceDate.markdownDescription:
Global cutoff date for commit extraction (YYYY-MM-DD format).

Commits before this date are ignored during the `gitCommitExtraction` pipeline step. This is useful for large repositories with many years of history where only recent commits are relevant.

**Priority**: Per-repository `startDate` settings override this global setting. When both are set, the *later* of the two dates is used.

**Examples:**
```json
// Extract commits from 2023 onwards
"gitrx.pipeline.sinceDate": "2023-01-01"

// No cutoff (extract all history)
"gitrx.pipeline.sinceDate": ""
```

gitrx.arcComponent.extensionMapping.description: Maps file extensions to architecture component categories for commit_files classification.
gitrx.arcComponent.extensionMapping.markdownDescription:
Maps file extensions to architecture component categories.

**Categories**: Front-End, Back-End, Database, DevOps/CI, Configuration, Documentation, Testing, Build/Tooling, Assets, Other

Multi-dot extensions (e.g., `.test.tsx`) take precedence over single-dot extensions (`.tsx`). Extension matching is case-insensitive.

Example: `{ ".proto": "Back-End", ".graphql": "Back-End" }`

After editing, re-run **Gitr: Backfill Architecture Components** to apply changes.

gitrx.arcComponent.filenameMapping.description: Maps specific filenames to architecture component categories. Filename matches take priority over extension matches.
gitrx.arcComponent.filenameMapping.markdownDescription:
Maps specific filenames (case-sensitive) to architecture component categories.

**Priority**: Filename matches always take precedence over extension matches.

**Categories**: Front-End, Back-End, Database, DevOps/CI, Configuration, Documentation, Testing, Build/Tooling, Assets, Other

Example: `{ "Procfile": "DevOps/CI", ".eslintrc": "Configuration" }`

After editing, re-run **Gitr: Backfill Architecture Components** to apply changes.

gitrx.charts.topNComplexFiles.description: Number of top complex files to display in the File Complexity chart
gitrx.charts.topNComplexFiles.markdownDescription:
Number of top complex files to display in the File Complexity chart. Must be between 1 and 100.

Default: `20`

gitrx.releaseManagement.productionBranch.description: Branch names considered as production environments for release tracking
gitrx.releaseManagement.productionBranch.markdownDescription:
Branch names considered as production environments for release tracking. Merges to these branches are counted as production releases.

Default: `["main", "master", "prod", "production"]`

Branches matching `release/*` patterns are also considered production.

gitrx.releaseManagement.stagingBranches.description: Branch names considered as staging/development environments for release tracking
gitrx.releaseManagement.stagingBranches.markdownDescription:
Branch names considered as staging/development environments for release tracking. Merges to these branches are counted as staging releases.

Default: `["staging", "stage", "uat", "develop", "dev"]`

gitrx.releaseManagement.defaultTimeRange.description: Default time range in days for the Release Management chart
gitrx.releaseManagement.defaultTimeRange.markdownDescription:
Default time range in days for the Release Management Contributions chart.

Default: `30`
